Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-22-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Justin A. Goodman, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman (415.956.8100) for Plaintiff STEVE LIN;
John P. McDonnell, Esq. (650.991.9909) for Defendant SUSAN D. TIMNEY

Defendants' motion is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SUSAN TIMNEY and LYNDA KRETLOW'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, this complaint does not assert an unlawful detainer, possession is not sought. Arguments advance for the first time in reply are not proper. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Second Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Jul-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Justin A. Goodman, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman (415.956.8100) for Plaintiff/Opposing Party STEVE LIN;
John P. McDonnell, Esq. (650.991.9909) for Defendant/Moving Party SUSAN D. TIMNEY

Plaintiff's counsel objects to the Court hearing defendant's motion in violation of CRC 3.1322(b). The Court hears arguments on the merits and will review CCP 425.16. Respective counsel address applicability of Feldman v. 1100 Park Lane (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1467 and litigation privilege to the intentional misrepresentation cause of action.

The Court issues the following tentative ruling:
Defendant's Special Motion to Strike is DENIED. Defendant failed to show that the second cause of action arises out of protected activity, even assuming that a response to the "3 Day Notice to Cure or Quit" is protected activity per Feldman v. 1100 Park Lane (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1467. Feldman Court found that the operative notice was "clearly connected to and logically related to the unlawful detainer action" and therefore its service fell under the CCP 425.16 protections. Moving party here failed to show that her statement regarding her residing at the Premises was "clearly connect to and logically related to unlawful detainer." Failure to reside at the premises was not the basis for the Notice to Cure or Quit. Timney does not provide any authority for the proposition that any statement made during the meeting that was arranged for the purposes of curing the Notice, are protected. In her declaration Timney does not claim that she made the alleged statements in response to the allegations in the Notice. Timney merely states that the statements were made "while I was [at] Attorney Bornstein's Office." Plaintiff provides a declaration showing that by the time the alleged statement was made, Notice to Cure or Quit has already been cured and therefore Timney's statement was not "preparatory to or in anticipation of the bringing of on an [unlawful detainer] action.

DEFENDANT SUSAN TIMNEY'S Special Motion To Strike Second Cause Of Action is taken under submission after the Court, has reviewed the pleadings submitted and having heard oral argument. Opposing counsel submits a proposed order tracking the Court's thoughts to the Court and to moving counsel. Plaintiff/Opposing party to comply with CRC 3.1312(b). The Court will review papers, argument, and sign counsel's order or the Court may prepare an order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (501)

Justin A. Goodman, of Zacks & Freedman, Tel. 415.956.8100, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances.

DEFENDANTS SUSAN TIMNEY AND LYNDA KRETLOW's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is transferred to Dept. 501, to be heard on June 10, 2015, at 9:30 a.m. The Anti-Slapp motion scheduled to be heard on June 10, 2015, in Dept. 302, is likewise transferred to Dept. 501.
